title: Childhood Autism Spectrum Test (CAST)
description: A 37-item parent/caregiver-report screening tool for identifying ASD traits in children aged 5-11 years. Based on Scott et al., 2002 and Baron-Cohen et al., 2009.

scoring.scale: 0 or 1 per item (Yes/No scoring — direction varies by question)

severity_levels.label: clinical
severity_levels.description: Above clinical threshold — comprehensive ASD assessment recommended

scoring.clinical_note: The CAST is a screening tool, not a diagnostic instrument. Scores ≥15 strongly suggest the need for a comprehensive ASD evaluation.

questions.text: Does your child join in playing games with other children easily?
Does your child come up to you spontaneously for a chat?
Was your child speaking by 2 years old?
Does your child enjoy sports?
Is it important to your child to fit in with the peer group?
Does your child appear to notice unusual details that others miss?
Does your child tend to take things literally?
When your child was 3 years old, did he/she spend a lot of time pretend playing (e.g., pretending to be a superhero, or holding teddy's tea parties)?
Does your child like to do things over and over again, in the same way all the time?
Does your child find it easy to interact with other children?
Can your child keep a two-way conversation going?
Can your child read appropriately for his/her age?
Does your child mostly have the same interests as his/her peers?
Does your child have an interest which takes up so much time that he/she does little else?
Does your child have friends, rather than just acquaintances?
Does your child often bring you things he/she is interested in to show you?
Does your child enjoy joking around?
Does your child have difficulty understanding the rules of polite behavior?
Does your child appear to have an unusual memory for details?
Is your child's voice unusual (e.g., overly adult, flat, or very monotonous)?
Are people important to your child?
Can your child dress him/herself?
Is your child good at turn-taking in conversation?
Does your child play imaginatively with other children, and engage in role-play?
Does your child often do or say things that are tactless or socially inappropriate?
Can your child count to 50 without leaving out any numbers?
Does your child make normal eye contact?
Does your child have any unusual and repetitive movements?
Is your child's social behaviour very one-sided and always on his/her own terms?
Does your child sometimes say 'you' or 'he/she' when he/she means 'I'?
Does your child prefer imaginative activities such as play-acting or story-telling, rather than numbers or lists of facts?
Does your child sometimes lose the listener because of not explaining what he/she is talking about?
Can your child ride a bicycle (even if with stabilizers)?
Does your child try to impose routines on him/herself, or on others, in such a way that it causes problems?
Does your child care how he/she is perceived by the rest of the group?
Does your child often turn conversations to his/her favourite subject rather than following what the other person wants to talk about?
Does your child have odd or unusual phrases?
